item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Tehran Hit by Heavy Airstrikes as U.S. Marines Arrive in Middle East
item.description: Airstrikes pummel residential areas and a university in Iran’s capital. Houthi forces in Yemen entered the widening conflict with a missile attack on Israel.

item.title: A Toothless Iran? Missile and Drone Strikes Show It Can Still Inflict Pain.
item.description: A wave of strikes across the Middle East in recent days shows that Iran has not lost the capacity to retaliate.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Saturday
item.description: The war with Iran escalated on Saturday as the Iran-allied Houthis joined the conflict and Israel bombarded Tehran with airstrikes.

item.title: Airstrikes batter a university and residential zones in Tehran.
Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
item.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib from the Hezbollah-affiliated Al Manar TV.

item.title: The Slum in Gangnam, the Richest Part of Seoul
item.description: The city wants to redevelop a shantytown in Gangnam district, where hundreds are defying eviction, fighting for a right to own a home in an area notorious for the exorbitant cost of housing.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
Ministers explore triggering break clause in Palantir’s NHS contract
item.description: Government is under pressure to eject the controversial American company from NHS England data systems

item.title: Eurozone borrowing costs soar on fears of fiscal hit from Iran shock
item.description: Government bonds face one of worst months of past decade as investors warn of ‘deterioration’ in public finances

item.title: The last bridge left to ancient Tyre
item.description: Evacuation orders and strikes turn once-vibrant Lebanese beach town into a refuge for holdouts and displaced

item.title: German chemicals groups boost prices as Iran war adds to industry woes
item.description: Companies worry about effects of higher energy costs even as they get short-term advantage over Asian peers

item.title: OpenAI investor says AI requires an income tax overhaul
item.description: Vinod Khosla says voter fears over technology causing job losses will shape upcoming US elections

item.title: UK risks losing defence tech start-ups to relocation amid funding delays
item.description: Slow progress on government contracts prompts some groups to consider moving abroad

item.title: Iran war chokes off helium supplies in threat to chipmakers and healthcare
item.description: Byproduct of natural gas is critical to the production of superconductors and the functioning of MRI scanners

item.title: Iran activates its ‘resistance economy’ to survive the war
item.description: Islamic republic has spent years building a model designed to withstand hostility even as it presides over a deepening economic malaise

item.title: The Reform UK youth wing driving the party’s reach on campus
item.description: To gain momentum, young supporters are trying to appeal to students from across the right

item.title: Police arrest man after car strikes ‘multiple pedestrians’ in Derby
item.description: A number of people were seriously injured in incident around 9.30pm on Saturday

item.title: Korea weighs inheritance tax based on book value, not market prices
item.description: Government proposal aims to curb the alleged suppression of share prices and reduce the ‘Korea discount’

item.title: Philippines ‘between rock and a hard place’ over surging energy prices
item.description: Central bank governor warns that raising rates to tackle inflation could hit already weak economic growth

item.title: Indian conglomerate Vedanta to split in five next month
item.description: Chair suggests new entities could be worth as much as $50bn after deleveraging drive

item.title: Halfway Through Lent, a Small Quebec Island Celebrates With Masks and Jigs
item.description: Few islanders still observe Lent, but they cling to a tradition once seen as defying the all-powerful Roman Catholic Church.

item.title: Schools do not have enough staff to make SEND reforms work, union warns
item.description: The National Education Union says schools need more funding to be able to make all classrooms inclusive.

item.title: One ant for $220: The new frontier of wildlife trafficking
item.description: The craze for collecting ants takes Kenya by surprise as smugglers zone in to make a profit.

item.title: How deepfake porn scandal surrounding TV star rocked Germany
item.description: Collien Fernandes has accused her ex-husband of spreading images of her online, but he has categorically denied it.

item.title: Shops and restaurants in Egypt told to close early as energy crisis deepens
item.description: Retail and dining premises will have to shut by 21:00 each night for the next month to conserve power.

item.title: In Dubai, a famed horse race goes on, despite the war.
Middle East war day 29 as it happened: Yemen’s Houthi rebels launch first attack on Israel since conflict began, Iranian strike injures US troops at Saudi air base
item.description: Rebels’ participation puts vital maritime routes at greater risk

item.title: Americans take to streets in ‘No Kings’ day protests against Trump
item.description: Latest series of marches comes against backdrop of president’s unpopular war in Iran

item.title: Support for JD Vance slips in straw poll of Republican candidates in 2028
item.description: US vice-president still favourite to succeed Donald Trump but secretary of state Marco Rubio is gaining ground

item.title: The Houthis’ attack on Israel heralds a significant escalation in the war with Iran
item.description: Yemen’s Shia militia could threaten to choke the Red Sea

item.title: Iran-backed Houthis join war with attack against Israel
item.description: There are fears the Iranian proxy may attack shipping in the Red Sea, adding to global economic woes.

item.title: Castro Heirs Emerge Across Cuba’s Political Scene Amid Energy Crisis and Trump Threats
item.description: As Trump officials demand changes, Castro family members are suddenly popping up across Cuba’s political scene. Some even ask: Could one be the “Cuban Delcy?”

item.title: F.B.I. Said to Dig Up Old Investigative Files on Democratic Lawmaker
item.description: The urgent instructions at the Trump administration’s behest to gather and relay the files on Representative Eric Swalwell have alarmed some career law enforcement officials.

item.title: Ukraine Finalizes Air Defense Deals With Gulf Nations Amid War in Mideast
item.description: The Ukrainian president visited the Middle East, where he sought to shift his country from a recipient of military aid to a supplier.

item.title: On Iran’s Rugged Frontier, Kurds Yearn to Join the Fight
item.description: These groups’ dream has long been to establish federal autonomy, akin to that of their fellow Kurds in Iraq. With Iran’s leaders battered and degraded, they hope their moment has come.

item.title: Two Cuba-bound aid ships found days after disappearing
item.description: The crew are safe and will complete their mission, a spokesman for the organisers said.

item.title: Man in Paris Accused of Attempted Attack on Bank of America
item.description: Prosecutors say the police thwarted an attacker as he was trying to detonate explosives. The episode is being investigated as possible terrorism.

item.title: Israeli Strike Kills 3 Journalists in Southern Lebanon, Officials Say
item.description: Israel accused one of the reporters of being a Hezbollah operative. Lebanon’s president said they were journalists and condemned the killings.

item.title: Potential Houthi threat to Red Sea shipping could further damage global economy
item.description: The Iran-backed group could bring a second crucial waterway to a standstill, writes Sebastian Usher.

item.title: Tears and defiance as Lebanese family bury child, 11, killed in Israeli strike
item.description: Jawad Younes and his uncle Ragheb were laid to rest on Saturday after their family compound was hit a day earlier.

item.title: Senegal parade Afcon trophy despite title controversy
item.description: Senegal parade the Africa Cup of Nations trophy before Saturday's friendly match against Peru in Paris - despite being stripped of the title earlier this month.

item.title: Yemen’s Houthis Fire at Israel and Vow Further Attacks
item.description: There has long been concern that the Houthis, an Iran-backed militia, could disrupt shipping in the Red Sea if they entered the broader war.

item.title: 'I was naive,' says minister who quit over Labour Together claims
item.description: Josh Simons resigned after facing claims a think tank he used to run commissioned a report into journalists' backgrounds.

item.title: UK and Singapore press ahead with digital deal as WTO remains deadlocked
item.description: Ministers are struggling to thrash out reforms at the global body that has been buffeted by Trump’s tariffs

item.title: Can This Russian Bakery Survive a 3,500% Tax Increase?
item.description: The challenges faced by a small-business owner near Moscow highlight how Russia’s war-drained economy is on the doorstep of a major crisis.

item.title: Twenty-two migrants die off Greek coast after six days at sea
item.description: Poor weather and a lack of food and water contributed to the deaths, the Greek coastguard says.

item.title: Pakistan Set to Host Top Regional Diplomats for Talks on Iran War
item.description: The foreign ministers of Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Egypt are expected in Islamabad on Sunday as the Pakistani government strives to find a diplomatic solution to the war.

item.title: Tourists cool on Cyprus and Turkey as Iran war upends travel plans
item.description: Popular destinations in eastern Mediterranean suffer amid ‘generalised anxiety’

item.title: Present Company
item.description: Entertaining out-of-town visitors can yield ideas for how to live more intentionally in your everyday life.

item.title: How long can Iran keep firing missiles?
item.description: Five analysts assess Tehran’s capacity to sustain barrages against Israel and Gulf states

item.title: Houthi missile attack signals ‘serious’ escalation in Iran war
item.description: Rebels’ participation puts vital maritime routes at greater risk

item.title: Nepal's ex-PM arrested over fatal protest crackdown
item.description: More than 70 people were killed during an uprising last year which ousted Oli's government.

item.title: Gaza peace doubts deepen as attention shifts to Iran
item.description: With the world focused on the Iran war, there is increasing uncertainty about what happens next for Gaza.

item.title: 'Our home is gone': BBC speaks to displaced families in Lebanon
item.description: The BBC's Hugo Bachega speaks to families in Beirut as more than a million people have been displaced in Lebanon.

item.title: A Mamdani Strategist’s Advice for Democrats in the 2026 Midterms
item.description: How to talk about affordability.

item.title: Video shows US missile likely used in deadly strike on Iranian town, experts say
item.description: The analysts identified a Precision Strike Missile (PrSM) in footage of the strike on the southern town of Lamerd.

item.title: From jammed broadcasts to a blocked website: BBC Russian's 80 years of defiance
item.description: For half a century, BBC Russian had to fend off jammers. Now its website is blocked, but millions still read it.

item.title: 'The gravest crime against humanity': What does the UN vote on slavery mean?
item.description: African and Caribbean nations want countries which benefited from slavery to pay compensation but it will not be straightforward.

item.title: Maldives tells UK it does not recognise Chagos Islands deal
item.description: The Maldives is threatening international legal action to press its claim for control of the Chagos Islands.

item.title: 'Memory of a generation': China mourns the sudden death of a controversial education influencer
item.description: The divisive 41-year-old was among the country's most prominent influencers.

item.title: I escaped North Korea with my mum. Now I'm terrified she might be sent back
item.description: Geumseong survived a dangerous journey to South Korea, but his mother is stuck in a Chinese prison and may be forcibly repatriated.

item.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
item.description: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

item.title: Offord says he is fit to be first minister after homophobic joke
item.description: Malcolm Offord denied being homophobic after apologising for a joke he made about George Michael during a Burns Night speech.

item.title: Screen time for under-fives should be limited to one hour a day, parents told
item.description: New government guidance also suggests children under-two should not be watching screens alone.

item.title: Austria becomes latest to propose social media ban for children
item.description: The proposed under-14 ban follows similar steps in other European countries, including France and Spain.

item.title: No evidence of 'family voting' in by-election
item.description: An investigation into alleged "family voting" finds no evidence of intent to influence voting.

item.title: Salah could play for seven more years - Klopp
item.description: Jurgen Klopp says Mohamed Salah could carry on playing until the age of 40 after the forward leaves Liverpool at the end of the season.

item.title: Reform candidate quits Senedd election after appearing to do Nazi salute
item.description: Corey Edwards and party leader Nigel Farage suggested he had been imitating Fawlty Towers character Basil Fawlty.

item.title: Vital paperwork for grooming gangs inquiry may be lost, say MPs
item.description: An independent inquiry into child sexual abuse by grooming gangs is about to begin its work.

item.title: Reform councillor suspended following racism row
item.description: Reform UK confirms the suspension of Lynn Dean and says an investigation is forthcoming.

item.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
item.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

item.title: Whale swims for freedom after big German rescue effort on Baltic coast
item.description: Rescue teams now believe the whale has reached deeper water in Lübeck Bay and hope it will head for the wider sea.

item.title: Thirteen arrested in joint Scotland and Spain gangland raids
item.description: Police Scotland and the Guardia Civil in Spain carried out simultaneous raids as part of a two-year probe into organised crime.

item.title: Assisted dying bill will not now become law, say both sides
item.description: The House of Lords are continuing to debate the proposals but there is not enough time to complete all the stages in the current parliamentary session.

item.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
item.description: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

item.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
item.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

item.title: Officials to ask Mandelson to hand over personal phone messages for release
item.description: Officials are set to publish more documents related to his appointment as UK ambassador to the US - but have so far only had access to the peer's work phone.

item.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
item.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

item.title: Spanish woman who died through euthanasia failed by state, say critics
item.description: Noelia Castillo died on Thursday evening in a Barcelona hospital, after a protracted legal battle with her own father.

item.title: Davey defends councils raising charges and taxes
item.description: Cost of living is a key campaign issue for the Liberal Democrats ahead of May's local elections.

item.title: Morocco ready to host Wafcon 'any time' as questions remain over postponement
item.description: A senior member of the Moroccan government says the country was “ready to host” the Women’s Africa Cup of Nations this month despite the decision to delay it until July.

item.title: Rationing power and diluting petrol - how African countries are coping with effects of Iran war
item.description: The disruption in oil supplies has caused shortages and concerns in several African countries.

item.title: Ukraine signs deal with Saudi Arabia offering drone expertise
item.description: Volodymyr Zelensky says the Gulf kingdom faces the same type of attacks from Iran that Kyiv has been resisting.

item.title: UAE and Qatar Arrest Hundreds Over Online Videos of Iranian Attacks
item.description: The authorities have made a wave of arrests to stop people posting footage of strikes, citing security risks. Experts also see a fear of damage to the countries’ image as safe havens.

item.title: South Africa's exclusion from G7 summit no surprise, says Ramaphosa
item.description: Reports say the US pressured France to rescind Ramaphosa’s invitation after Trump threatened to boycott the summit.

item.title: Two hereditary peers allowed to keep ceremonial roles
item.description: The Duke of Norfolk and Lord Carrington won the concession due to their role in organising state occasions.

item.title: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
item.description: Women in Kenya and Ghana tell the BBC about being approached by a Russian man who later posts videos of them without their consent.

item.title: Nepal swears in ex-rapper as new prime minister
item.description: Balendra Shah won the election by a landslide promising change to Nepalis who are angry at corruption.

item.title: Nepal’s New Prime Minister, Former Rapper Balendra Shah, Sworn In
item.description: A leaked report on the deadly violence and mass arson that broke out last year has put the new leader under pressure to ensure accountability.

item.title: Singapore denies Malaysian scholar entry, calling her 'undesirable visitor'
item.description: Fadiah Nadwa Fikri urged some in Singapore to "adopt her brand of radical advocacy", authorities say.

item.title: How Russia's threat has seen Germany become Europe's most important army
item.description: Germany once terrorised Europe, but now Europe is welcoming a resurgent German military.

item.title: Belarus leader gifts rifle to North Korea's Kim as they sign friendship treaty
item.description: Two key supporters of Russia's war in Ukraine close ranks as Alexander Lukashenko visits Pyongyang.

item.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
item.description: The price of a wide range of goods - from food, to smartphones, to medicines - could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

item.title: What happened at Maduro's second court appearance
item.description: The BBC was in the New York courtroom to watch the ex-Venezuelan leader argue for ability to fund his defence with Venezuelan government funds.

item.title: Donald Trump Is Breaking Up with Europe
item.description: And the war in Iran is helping him do it.

item.title: Farage backs election candidate appearing to do Nazi salute in photo
item.description: Farage says he would not approve of the picture but would not kick him out of the party.

item.title: More than 13 million living in poverty, new figures show
item.description: The level rose slightly on the previous year while the number of children living in poverty has remained steady.

item.title: Farage says local elections are referendum on Starmer, as he launches campaign
item.description: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage is launching his party's push to take control of more councils at a rally in Sunderland.

item.title: 'Dedicated' former MP dies aged 92
item.description: David Winnick is described as a "true legend" and "dedicated" public servant following his death.

item.title: US prosecutors argue Maduro 'plundered' Venezuelan wealth in court battle over legal fees
item.description: A judge said the right to defence was paramount as prosecutors argued the couple should not be able to use Venezuelan funds to pay lawyers.

item.title: Fifa investigates Congolese FA boss jailed alongside wife and son
item.description: Fecofoot president Jean-Guy Mayolas remains on the run after being sentenced to life imprisonment earlier this month for embezzling $1.1m in Fifa funds.

item.title: Orban's Hungarian government accused of mass voter intimidation ahead of election
item.description: A film quoting voters, mayors and a police officer alleges money and drugs are being offered to pressure people to back the ruling party.

item.title: UK now 'ready' to seize Russian shadow fleet ships, Healey says
item.description: Forces can now board sanctioned vessels if required, after ministers identified a legal basis in January.

item.title: Why was a £1.5bn Chinese plan to invest in a Highland yard blocked?
item.description: Ming Yang proposed building a wind turbine manufacturing plant at Ardersier.

item.title: Israel says it has killed Iran's navy chief overseeing Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.description: Israel's defence minister said a number of other "senior Navy command officials" have also been killed. Iran has not yet commented.

item.title: King's Speech to take place days after key May elections
item.description: King Charles III will attend Parliament to read out the government's legislative agenda for the coming months.

item.title: French court hands Islam scholar Tariq Ramadan 18-year jail term for rape
item.description: Ramadan, a 63-year-old former Oxford professor, denied the charges but his lawyers said he was too ill to attend the trial.

item.title: Why does the US have Iran's Kharg Island in its sights?
item.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

item.title: Russia wants to limit contact with the outside world
item.description: A recent internet blackout reflects the Kremlin’s nervousness

item.title: Germany’s Social Democrats gaze into the abyss
item.description: But an electoral drubbing could open a window for national reforms

item.title: France offers some hope for defeating populists
item.description: The mainstream parties get the biggest prizes

item.title: Iran’s regime walls off the internet
item.description: The latest shutdown looks designed to be permanent

item.title: Israeli settlers are growing more violent in the West Bank
item.description: Since the war with Iran began, attacks against Palestinians have become more systematic

item.title: In the current Gulf war, water may prove as decisive as oil
item.description: The Arab states are plainly vulnerable, but so is Iran

item.title: How Chinese companies are reshaping Indonesia
item.description: They are fleeing cut-throat competition at home

item.title: Millions of Burmese struggle to find safety in Thailand
item.description: Many refugees are left exposed

item.title: China is breaking into one of the world’s weirdest car markets
item.description: An island of American motoring in Central Asia is changing fast

item.title: Brazil has a secret weapon against oil shocks
item.description: Biofuels will help the country fend off the effects of conflict in the Middle East

item.title: Mexico’s broken economy
item.description: Claudia Sheinbaum’s government is tinkering, not reforming

item.title: China’s huge pork industry is a victim of its own success
item.description: The country is awash in its favourite meat

item.title: Does the Iran war increase the risk of a Chinese attack on Taiwan?
item.description: China has reason to wait, but its window of opportunity has widened

item.title: Jiang Shengnan is the most vocal woman in Chinese politics
item.description: She is trying to push change from within the system

item.title: England has shown the world how to replace farm subsidies
item.description: A rare Brexit dividend

item.title: Far-fetched to think McSweeney faked phone theft, says PM
item.description: Messages relating to Lord Peter Mandelson's appointment as US ambassador could have been lost in the alleged theft.

item.title: Speaker urged to stop Keir Starmer avoiding MPs' questions
item.description: The public are growing tired of Sir Keir Starmer's conduct at PMQs, according to letters obtained by the BBC.

item.title: Russia bans Oscar-winning film 'Mr Nobody Against Putin'
item.description: A court alleges that the documentary promoted "negative attitudes" about the Russian government and the war in Ukraine.

item.title: European Parliament gives conditional approval to EU-US trade deal
item.description: The agreement would see EU tariffs on US goods reduced to zero while US tariffs on EU imports set at 15%.

item.title: How long will Israel stay in Lebanon?
item.description: The threat of another occupation is tearing at the fabric of Lebanese society

item.title: The end of the world’s longest-running Maoist insurgency
item.description: A report from one of the Naxalites’ final redoubts

item.title: Donald Trump says he is close to a deal with Iran
item.description: Almost no one believes that talks are getting anywhere

item.title: Advantage Iran
item.description: A month of bombing has achieved nothing. Will Donald Trump escalate, or talk?

item.title: Europe should think twice before weakening its merger rules
item.description: A strict competition policy is not the barrier to bigger firms

item.title: The case against energy bail-outs
item.description: As war rages in Iran, governments must not repeat the mistakes of 2022

item.title: New India bill to amend transgender rights sparks protests
item.description: The bill seeks to change how transgender people are legally recognised and their right to self-identify.

item.title: 'We are being choked': Philippines transport workers strike over fuel costs
item.description: It comes as a ship carrying barrels of Russian crude oil arrived in the Philippines earlier this week.
